project_overview.project_development_objective: To facilitate a sustainable supply of gas through targeted investments in gas infrastructure and enhanced gas sector governance
project_overview.beneficiaries: Government agencies (MoMP, AOGRA, AGE), electricity consumers in North-East Power System (NEPS), IPP investors, civil society organizations, AEITI

geographic_scope.coverage_type: national with regional focus on northern Afghanistan
geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: yes - project area reported to be insecure with high level of insurgency activity; however, northwestern part of Afghanistan has limited instances of violence in last 10-15 years; overall security risk within country remains high with civilian casualties at 10,993 in 2018

key_components.description: Support for gas infrastructure development including completion of Sheberghan-Mazar Pipeline (SMPL), optimization of field facilities, procurement and installation of new amine plant, and technical assistance for Yatimtaq field development and Totimaidan gas block tender

key_components.component_name: Component B: Strengthening Gas Sector Governance
key_components.description: Institutional capacity building for MoMP and AOGRA including development of regulatory framework, human resource development, environmental and social management capacity, transparency initiatives (AEITI), and grievance redress mechanisms
Project management support including recruitment of project management support firm, incremental operating costs, equipment, training, and project audits

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Ministry of Mines and Petroleum (MoMP) as primary implementing agency with Afghan Gas Enterprise (AGE) and Afghanistan Oil and Gas Regulatory Authority (AOGRA) as beneficiary agencies
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Project Management Team within MoMP including Deputy Minister as Senior Project Director, project coordinator, procurement specialist, FM specialist, M&E specialist, environmental and social specialists; Project Implementation Teams (PITs) in AGE and AOGRA; Memoranda of Understanding (MoUs) between MoMP and beneficiary agencies; coordination with other ministries (MEW, MoF, NEPA, MoLSAD, Ministry of Interior, Ministry of Transport, Ministry of Urban Development, MoIC)
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: World Bank Procurement Regulations for IPF Borrowers (July 2016, revised November 2017 and August 2018); Project Procurement Strategy for Development (PPSD) developed; use of STEP system; open international competition for large contracts; national procurement procedures for some activities; market engagement activities for high-value items; direct payment method for large foreign currency contracts
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: MoMP responsible for overall M&E based on Results Framework indicators; quarterly progress reports within 45 days of quarter close; biannual reviews starting 6 months after effectiveness; Supervision Engineer reports for pipeline construction; third-party monitoring for compensation payments; gender-disaggregated data collection; monitoring of E&S safeguards implementation

identified_risks.risk_description: Role and reach of government and nongovernment actors, political and cultural affiliations, entrenched practices along hydrocarbon value chain, political instability and transition, uncertainty from September 2019 presidential elections
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Established oversight and financial monitoring systems at MoMP; transparency principles preventing fraud; strengthened contract monitoring and review processes; support to EITI Secretariat and MSG for reconciliation and publication of payments; approval of Mining Sector Road Map by HEC; continued support for AOGRA establishment

identified_risks.risk_description: Afghanistan heavily reliant on aid; reduction in security and civilian support would pressure fiscal sustainability; uncertainty regarding continued external support
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Implementation of reforms under Incentive Program Development Policy Grants; Public-Private Partnerships and Public Investment Advisory Project to promote private financing; focus on structural reforms

identified_risks.risk_description: Beneficiary agencies' cyclical implementation capacity and weak coordination; delays in pipeline construction affecting gas deliverability to Mazar IPP; delays in Supervision Engineer work; risks around timely procurement of equipment not available in Afghanistan; coordination challenges among MoMP, AGE, MEW; risk that GoA budget allocation to AGE may not fully materialize
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Project streamlined from original design; capacity building and TA by international technical experts; accelerated and exceptional procurement processes; Government commitment to allocating budget to AGE and addressing insecurity; US$1 million contingency for civil works and labor costs; project management firm to support coordination and resolve bottlenecks

identified_risks.risk_description: MoMP undergoes cyclical structural and staffing changes weakening institutional capacity; frequent changes to core leadership; limited capacity of institutions managing hydrocarbon sector; loss of institutional memory and trained staff
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Reinvigorated approach to assistance with less reliance on international experts; emphasis on MoMP and government agencies in project implementation; support for young professionals program; participation in TAGHIR program for staff capacity and performance; establishment of document archive through PMSF; substantial TA on O&M for gas infrastructure; grounding TA in concrete investments

identified_risks.risk_description: Very low capacity within MoMP on procurement, FM, contract management; procurement process often delayed and lengthy; consultants not paid for various reasons; poor contract management; low capacity for managing large contracts (largest previous contracts under US$1.8 million); weak internal audit capacity; risk of asset ownership and delivery details not properly documented
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Procurement of project management support firm within 8 months of effectiveness to provide capacity building and enhance professional skills; MoU between MoMP and beneficiary agencies to define implementation arrangements; disbursement conditions requiring detailed design and engineering; recruitment of national internal audit specialist within 3 months; use of Financial Management Manual developed by World Bank; development of financial and document management software; use of country FM systems; quarterly interim unaudited financial reports; annual audited financial statements by Supreme Audit Office

identified_risks.risk_description: Low capacity for E&S management by responsible agencies; MoMP's E&S capacity declined over recent years; previously established E&S unit became non-operational; downstream E&S risks associated with TA; potential impacts from future gas production activities; land acquisition impacts from pipeline and field development; labor influx risks; GBV risks; security concerns for project workers and equipment; limited capacity for stakeholder engagement
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Comprehensive E&S documents prepared including ESMF, Resettlement Framework, Labor Management Procedure, Stakeholder Engagement Plan; Environmental and Social Audit and Ex-post Social Audit for completed 44 km of pipeline; ESIA, ESMP, and RP to be prepared before commencement of remaining construction; E&S Unit established within MoMP with qualified personnel; substantial capacity building for E&S management; third-party monitoring for compensation payments; GRM established with dedicated GRM Unit; code of conduct for GBV prevention; security plan to be developed before civil works; community health and safety plans required; meaningful stakeholder consultations conducted

identified_risks.risk_description: Civil society and media hampered with misinformation and misunderstanding of sector developments; lack of awareness or sensationalism of expected outcomes; array of stakeholders affected by hydrocarbon development
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Comprehensive Stakeholder Engagement Plan implemented; strengthening of AEITI MSG and consultative working groups; participation in extractive industries coordination groups; improved community engagement; establishment and implementation of GRM for gas infrastructure development; capacity building and training for GRM Committee members and relevant communities; citizen engagement and social accountability initiatives; active coordination with development partners

identified_risks.risk_description: High overall security risk within Afghanistan; civilian casualties at 10,993 in 2018; violence increased in August and September 2019; project area reported to be insecure with high level of insurgency activity; security has been impediment to AGE's ability to complete pipeline construction
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Government commitment to allocating budget and security personnel to ensure pipeline construction not impeded; AGE assessed as best option to construct pipeline due to understanding of security environment and community relationships; continuous monitoring of security situation by World Bank in collaboration with Government; necessary corrective action to be taken as situation evolves

triggered_safeguard_policies: Assessment and Management of Environmental and Social Risks and Impacts (ESS1)
Resource Efficiency and Pollution Prevention and Management (ESS3)
Land Acquisition, Restrictions on Land Use and Involuntary Resettlement (ESS5)
Biodiversity Conservation and Sustainable Management of Living Natural Resources (ESS6)
safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: yes - Project-level GRM established with dedicated GRM Unit staffed with GRM Unit manager and grievance focal point for gas pipeline and amine plant; GRM Committee to address project-related grievances; target of 70% of project-related grievances addressed within one month; separate GBV GRM system potentially run by GBV services provider with feedback to project GRM; workers informed of GRM at time of recruitment; GRM guidance note included in Stakeholder Engagement Plan; World Bank Grievance Redress Service and Inspection Panel also available
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Project rated as having gender considerations; desk review identified gender disparities in extractives sector; only 12% of MoMP employees are women (4.8% contracted, 7.2% government officials); only 2 women among 12 AOGRA staff hired; no more than 2,000 women enrolled in public universities in engineering/petroleum/energy fields; project includes activities to increase women's employment from 12% to 20% in MoMP; young professionals program targeting women and men; skills training for existing women staff; gender sensitization training; Gender Unit of MoMP to be strengthened; code of conduct for GBV prevention in extractives sector; gender action plan to be developed during implementation; project rated Moderate risk under GBV infrastructure assessment tool
vulnerable_groups: Project-affected families (8 families at Qasim Abad village affected by pipeline, already compensated)
Communities in project area with limited economic opportunities
Laborers from outside project area (potential influx risks)
Poor and vulnerable populations in rural areas (82% of poor live in rural areas)

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project acknowledges fragile and conflict-affected context of Afghanistan; security concerns are real as some project sites are in conflict zones under insurgents' influence; project area reported to be insecure with high level of insurgency activity; project design includes security risk mitigation through Government commitment to allocating security personnel; use of AGE for pipeline construction due to their understanding of security environment and community relationships; continuous monitoring of security situation; project activities designed to support energy security and diversification which can contribute to stability; gas-to-power development intended to increase energy access and security, reducing reliance on imports; project includes stakeholder engagement and grievance mechanisms to address community concerns
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Afghanistan classified as Fragile State; economic growth slowed substantially with reduction in international troop numbers and increased insecurity since 2014; civilian casualties at unprecedented levels (10,459 killed/wounded in 2017, 10,993 in 2018); poverty rate increased from 38% (2011/12) to 55% (2016/17); drought-induced displacement reached record 298,582 individuals; weak labor demand despite increasing labor force; security-related constraints on service delivery; government heavily reliant on aid; macroeconomic outlook subject to high risks; country remains in transition with uncertain political settlement prospects; institutional capacity remains limited despite recent improvements; frequent changes in government leadership affecting institutional continuity
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Overall security risk rated High; civilian casualties remain high despite 27% decrease in first half of 2019; violence increased in August and September 2019; project area in northern Afghanistan reported to be insecure with high level of insurgency activity; security has been impediment to AGE's ability to complete pipeline construction; project sites in relatively stable northwestern part of Afghanistan with limited violence in last 10-15 years but overall country security risk remains high; deployment of security personnel required for protection of project workers and equipment; security plan to be developed before civil works; Government commitment to allocating security personnel and addressing insecurity in project area; World Bank to continuously monitor security situation and take corrective action
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: yes - Drought-induced displacement reached record levels of 298,582 individuals, mainly to urban areas in adjacent provinces; project-specific land acquisition impacts identified: 8 families at Qasim Abad village affected by first 44 km of pipeline construction, already compensated; potential future land acquisition impacts from remaining 45.1 km of pipeline, new amine plant, and field development activities (drilling of additional wells, gas platforms, gathering pipelines, service and access roads); Resettlement Framework prepared to guide management of land acquisitions; Resettlement Action Plan to be prepared for remaining pipeline section and new amine plant before commencement of construction; land can likely be obtained through land lease method as facilities expected to be on government-owned land; Ex-post Social Audit prepared for completed works including compensation details; Third Party Monitoring engaged for monitoring and reporting on compensation payments
